RPS10 SOD Experiments

2024-05-21

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "~/Documents/Postdoc/SOD_Microbiome")
library(phyloseq); packageVersion("phyloseq")

theme_set(theme_cowplot())

Load data

load("Data/RPS10/filtered_data/sod_pruned.RData")
load("Data/RPS10/filtered_data/sod_prunereads_5asv.RData")

filtered_read_counts<-read.csv("Data/RPS10/filtered_data/RPS10_subset_track_reads_sod.csv")
summary(filtered_read_counts)
##       X                 input           filtered        denoisedF     
##  Length:98          Min.   :   479   Min.   :    26   Min.   :     6  
##  Class :character   1st Qu.: 20450   1st Qu.:  6715   1st Qu.:  6704  
##  Mode  :character   Median : 41878   Median : 15740   Median : 15625  
##                     Mean   : 49046   Mean   : 22382   Mean   : 22324  
##                     3rd Qu.: 67817   3rd Qu.: 28897   3rd Qu.: 28861  
##                     Max.   :280013   Max.   :185457   Max.   :185346  
##    denoisedR          merged          nonchim      
##  Min.   :     2   Min.   :     2   Min.   :     2  
##  1st Qu.:  6692   1st Qu.:  6486   1st Qu.:  6486  
##  Median : 15586   Median : 14583   Median : 14582  
##  Mean   : 22313   Mean   : 20821   Mean   : 20646  
##  3rd Qu.: 28856   3rd Qu.: 25856   3rd Qu.: 25367  
##  Max.   :185244   Max.   :175106   Max.   :175106
new_names <- sapply(strsplit(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps@sam_data$Sample_nameRps10, "-"), function(x) {
  substr(x[1], 3, nchar(x[1]))
})

sod_prunereads_5asv.ps@sam_data$Sample_nameRps10 <- new_names

sod_prunereads.ps@sam_data$Fire_Mod <- factor(sod_prunereads.ps@sam_data$Fire_Mod, 
                                              levels = c("untreated","SODtrt","wildfire"), 
                                                  labels=c("Untreated","Treated","Wildfire"))

sod_prunereads_5asv.ps@sam_data$Fire_Mod <- factor(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps@sam_data$Fire_Mod, 
                                              levels = c("untreated","SODtrt","wildfire"), 
                                                  labels=c("Untreated","Treated","Wildfire"))

Bar plot agglomerated by order-facetted by fire factor

RPS10data <- sod_prunereads_5asv.ps %>%
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x / sum(x)}) %>% 
  tax_glom(taxrank = "Order") %>% 
  psmelt() %>% 
  #mutate(across(Kingdom:Species, ~ ifelse(is.na(.), "Unclassified", .))) %>%  
  arrange(desc(Abundance))

abund_plot <- ggplot(RPS10data, aes(x = Sample_nameRps10, y = Abundance, fill = Order)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black", size = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d() +
  scale_color_viridis_d() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    y = "Relative Abundance",
    fill = "Order"
  ) +
  facet_grid(~factor(Fire_Mod), scales = "free_x") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 20),  # Increase y-axis label size
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),  # Remove the x-axis title if needed
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20), 
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    legend.key.size = unit(1, "lines"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24, face= "bold"),
    strip.background = element_blank()
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(
      reverse = FALSE,
      keywidth = 1,
      keyheight = 1,
      title.position = "top",
      title.hjust = 0.5,
      label.theme = element_text(size = 20)
    )
  )
## Warning in geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black", :
## Ignoring unknown parameters: `size`
abund_plot <- abund_plot + 
  scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) gsub("Sample_nameRps10", "", x))  # Remove unwanted text

abund_plot

# Save the plot
ggsave("Figures/RPS10/relabundance_plot_fire_relabund_order_wildfire_samples.pdf", abund_plot, width = 24, height = 10)

Let’s summarize with boxplot

dataRPS10 <- sod_prunereads_5asv.ps %>%
  tax_glom(taxrank = "Genus") %>% 
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x / sum(x)}) %>% 
  psmelt() %>% 
  filter(Abundance > 0.05)

combined_plot <- ggplot(dataRPS10, aes(x = Fire_Mod, y = Abundance, fill = Genus)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.5) +  # Boxplot with no outliers and some transparency
  geom_jitter(color = "black", width = 0.2, size = 2) +  # Jittered black dots
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option="H") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    y = "Relative Abundance"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 24),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 24),
    legend.position = "none",  # Remove the legend
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24, face= "bold"),
    strip.background = element_blank()
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Genus, scales = "free_y") +
  stat_compare_means(method = "anova", label.y = 40)

# Save the plot
ggsave("Figures/RPS10/relabundance_plot_combined_facet_boxplot_genus_wildfire_samples.pdf", combined_plot, width = 30, height = 16)

Let’s look top taxa at family level

top40 <- names(sort(taxa_sums(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps), decreasing=TRUE))[1:40]
ps.top40 <- transform_sample_counts(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps, function(OTU) OTU/sum(OTU))
#ps.top40@tax_table[is.na(ps.top40@tax_table)] <- "Unclassified"
ps.top40 <- tax_glom(ps.top40, taxrank = "Family")
ps.top40 <- prune_taxa(top40, ps.top40)

pdf("Figures/RPS10/top40_family_sod_wildfire_samples.pdf", width = 34, height = 20)
plot_bar(ps.top40, x="Sample_nameRps10", fill="Family") + facet_wrap(~Treatuntreat_EP, scales="free_x")
## Warning: `aes_string()` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.0.0.
## ℹ Please use tidy evaluation idioms with `aes()`.
## ℹ See also `vignette("ggplot2-in-packages")` for more information.
## ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the phyloseq package.
##   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/joey711/phyloseq/issues>.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2

Let’s look top taxa at genus level

top40 <- names(sort(taxa_sums(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps), decreasing=TRUE))[1:40]
ps.top40 <- transform_sample_counts(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps, function(OTU) OTU/sum(OTU))
#ps.top40@tax_table[is.na(ps.top40@tax_table)] <- "Unclassified"
ps.top40 <- tax_glom(ps.top40, taxrank = "Genus")
ps.top40 <- prune_taxa(top40, ps.top40)

pdf("Figures/RPS10/top40_genus_sod_wildfire_samples.pdf", width = 34, height = 20)
plot_bar(ps.top40, x="Sample_nameRps10", fill="Genus") + facet_wrap(~Treatuntreat_EP, scales="free_x")
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2

Make data table of top taxa

data <-
  sod_prunereads_5asv.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Species") %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed <- data %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Fire_Mod, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance),
            Prevalence = n_distinct(Sample)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Total_Abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = 100*(Abundance / Total_Abundance))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Fire_Mod', 'Kingdom', 'Phylum', 'Class',
## 'Order', 'Family', 'Genus'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
write.csv(data_summed, file="Results/RPS10/mostabundanttaxa_abundance_species_wildfire_samples.csv",row.names = TRUE)

data1 <-
  sod_prunereads_5asv.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Genus") %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed1 <- data1 %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Fire_Mod, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance),
            Prevalence = n_distinct(Sample)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Total_Abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = 100*(Abundance / Total_Abundance))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Fire_Mod', 'Phylum', 'Class', 'Order',
## 'Family'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
write.csv(data_summed1, file="Results/RPS10/mostabundanttaxa_abundance_genus_wildfire_samples.csv",row.names = TRUE)

Let’s look at spacial placement of different plots

pdf("Figures/RPS10/location_of_plots_wildfire_samples.pdf", width = 20, height = 12)
map_plot <- ggplot(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps@sam_data, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude)) +
  geom_text(aes(color = Fire_Mod, label = Sample_nameRps10), size = 2.5, 
            check_overlap = TRUE) +
  labs(x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude", title = "Sampling Locations") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20), 
    axis.title = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12)
  )
print(map_plot)
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2

Let’s normalize our data in preparation for beta diversity analyses

sod_prunereads_5asv.ps<- phyloseq::prune_taxa(taxa_sums(sod_prunereads.ps) > 5, sod_prunereads.ps)
sdt = data.table::data.table(as(sample_data(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps), "data.frame"),
                 TotalReads = sample_sums(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps), keep.rownames = TRUE)
min_reads<-min(sdt$TotalReads)

rarefied.ps <- rarefy_even_depth(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps, sample.size = min_reads, rngseed = 100)
## `set.seed(100)` was used to initialize repeatable random subsampling.
## Please record this for your records so others can reproduce.
## Try `set.seed(100); .Random.seed` for the full vector
## ...
## 26OTUs were removed because they are no longer 
## present in any sample after random subsampling
## ...
strat_rel.ps<-transform_sample_counts(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps, function(x) x / sum(x))

Alpha diversity-use non-normalized or pruned data

Let’s start with t-test as point of comparison with anova analysis

set.seed(1)
AD <- estimate_richness(sod_prunereads.ps, measures = c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon"))
rownames(AD) <- gsub("\\.", "-", rownames(AD))

AD <- cbind(AD, sample_data(sod_prunereads.ps)[row.names(AD), c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Lineage_EP", "SoilBin", "VegBin")])

AD <- data.table(AD, keep.rownames = TRUE)

AD <- melt(AD, id.vars = c('rn', c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Lineage_EP", "SoilBin", "VegBin")))

factors <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Lineage_EP", "SoilBin", "VegBin")
ttest_list <- list()

for (factor_name in factors) {
  ttest <- data.table(alpha_index = character(), pvalue = numeric())
  for (alphad in c("InvSimpson", "Shannon", "Observed")) {
    ttest_res <- t.test(value ~ get(factor_name), data = AD[variable == alphad], var.equal = TRUE)
    p_value <- ttest_res$p.value
    ttest <- rbind(ttest, data.table(alpha_index = alphad, pvalue = p_value))
  }
  
  ttest_list[[factor_name]] <- ttest
}

for (factor_name in factors) {
  cat(paste("T-Test Results for Factor:", factor_name), "\n")
  print(knitr::kable(ttest_list[[factor_name]], format = "markdown"))
  cat("\n")
}
## T-Test Results for Factor: Treatuntreat_EP 
## 
## 
## |alpha_index |    pvalue|
## |:-----------|---------:|
## |InvSimpson  | 0.9941577|
## |Shannon     | 0.9203749|
## |Observed    | 0.9596284|
## 
## T-Test Results for Factor: Lineage_EP 
## 
## 
## |alpha_index |    pvalue|
## |:-----------|---------:|
## |InvSimpson  | 0.0195298|
## |Shannon     | 0.0155486|
## |Observed    | 0.2157685|
## 
## T-Test Results for Factor: SoilBin 
## 
## 
## |alpha_index |    pvalue|
## |:-----------|---------:|
## |InvSimpson  | 0.4802264|
## |Shannon     | 0.4035668|
## |Observed    | 0.7961629|
## 
## T-Test Results for Factor: VegBin 
## 
## 
## |alpha_index |    pvalue|
## |:-----------|---------:|
## |InvSimpson  | 0.0523886|
## |Shannon     | 0.0642351|
## |Observed    | 0.4607900|

Let’s now proceed with ANOVA analyses and then compile our alpha diversity stats

set.seed(1)
anova_list <- list()

AD <- estimate_richness(sod_prunereads.ps, measures = c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon"))
rownames(AD) <- gsub("\\.", "-", rownames(AD))

AD <- cbind(AD, sample_data(sod_prunereads.ps)[row.names(AD), c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")])

AD <- data.table(AD, keep.rownames = TRUE)

AD <- melt(AD, id.vars = c('rn', c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")))

factors2 <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")

plotted_factors <- character()
 
final_anova_table <- data.table(factor_name = character(), alpha_index = character(), pvalue = numeric(), df_between = numeric(), df_within = numeric(), ss_between = numeric(), ss_within = numeric(), ms_between = numeric(), ms_within = numeric(), F_value = numeric())

for (factor_name in factors2) {
  anova <- data.table(factor_name = character(), alpha_index = character(), pvalue = numeric(), df_between = numeric(), df_within = numeric(), ss_between = numeric(), ss_within = numeric(), ms_between = numeric(), ms_within = numeric(), F_value = numeric())
  
  for (alphad in c("Observed","InvSimpson", "Shannon")) {
    anova_res <- aov(value ~ get(factor_name), data = AD[variable == alphad])
    anova_summary <- summary(anova_res)
    p_value <- anova_summary[[1]]$`Pr(>F)`[1]
    df_between <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Df"[1]
    df_within <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Df"[2]
    ss_between <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Sum Sq"[1]
    ss_within <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Sum Sq"[2]
    ms_between <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Mean Sq"[1]
    ms_within <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Mean Sq"[2]
    F_value <- anova_summary[[1]]$"F value"[1]
    
    anova <- rbind(anova, data.table(factor_name = factor_name, alpha_index = alphad, pvalue = p_value, df_between = df_between, df_within = df_within, ss_between = ss_between, ss_within = ss_within, ms_between = ms_between, ms_within = ms_within, F_value = F_value))
  }
  
  final_anova_table <- rbind(final_anova_table, anova)
}

print(knitr::kable(final_anova_table, format = "markdown"))
## 
## 
## |factor_name     |alpha_index |    pvalue| df_between| df_within|  ss_between|  ss_within| ms_between|  ms_within|   F_value|
## |:---------------|:-----------|---------:|----------:|---------:|-----------:|----------:|----------:|----------:|---------:|
## |Treatuntreat_EP |Observed    | 0.9596284|          1|        85|   0.0830648| 2739.25027|  0.0830648| 32.2264737| 0.0025775|
## |Treatuntreat_EP |InvSimpson  | 0.9941577|          1|        85|   0.0001495|  235.54838|  0.0001495|  2.7711574| 0.0000539|
## |Treatuntreat_EP |Shannon     | 0.9203749|          1|        85|   0.0033196|   28.07128|  0.0033196|  0.3302504| 0.0100518|
## |Fire_Mod        |Observed    | 0.0612174|          2|        84| 176.2604588| 2563.07287| 88.1302294| 30.5127723| 2.8883062|
## |Fire_Mod        |InvSimpson  | 0.0689816|          2|        84|  14.5287255|  221.01980|  7.2643627|  2.6311881| 2.7608679|
## |Fire_Mod        |Shannon     | 0.0115994|          2|        84|   2.8264974|   25.24811|  1.4132487|  0.3005727| 4.7018533|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR   |Observed    | 0.4544220|          4|        82| 118.0864662| 2621.24687| 29.5216165| 31.9664252| 0.9235195|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR   |InvSimpson  | 0.6038387|          4|        82|   7.6244753|  227.92405|  1.9061188|  2.7795616| 0.6857624|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR   |Shannon     | 0.8616639|          4|        82|   0.4357690|   27.63883|  0.1089422|  0.3370590| 0.3232142|
write.csv(final_anova_table, "Results/RPS10/final_anova_table_wildfire_samples.csv", row.names = FALSE)

Let’s make faceted boxplots for our alpha diversity analyses

set.seed(1)
AD <- estimate_richness(sod_prunereads.ps, measures = c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon"))
rownames(AD) <- gsub("\\.", "-", rownames(AD))

AD <- cbind(AD, sample_data(sod_prunereads_5asv.ps)[rownames(AD), c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")])

variables <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")

plots <- list()

for (variable in variables) {
  p <- plot_richness(sod_prunereads.ps, x = variable, measures = c("Shannon", "InvSimpson", "Observed")) + 
    geom_boxplot()
  plots[[variable]] <- p
}

plots_arrange <- gridExtra::grid.arrange(
  plots[["Treatuntreat_EP"]], plots[["Fire_Mod"]], plots[["YrSinceTrt_BR"]],ncol=1)

plots_arrange_with_text <- cowplot::ggdraw() +
  cowplot::draw_plot(plots_arrange) +
  cowplot::draw_plot("Treatuntreat_EP", x = 0.28, y = 0.98) +
  cowplot::draw_plot("Fire_EP", x = 0.28, y = 0.98) +
  cowplot::draw_plot("YrSinceTrt_BR", x = 0.28, y = 0.98) 
## Warning in as_grob.default(plot): Cannot convert object of class character into
## a grob.
## Warning in as_grob.default(plot): Cannot convert object of class character into
## a grob.
## Warning in as_grob.default(plot): Cannot convert object of class character into
## a grob.
ggsave(plots_arrange_with_text, path = "Figures/RPS10", filename = "alpha_diversity_each_factor_sod_wildfire_samples.pdf", 
       width = 16, height = 24)

Let’s perform TUKEY HSD tests

# Define your variables of interest
set.seed(1)
variables <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")
measures <- c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon")

tukey_results <- list()

for (variable in variables) {
  for (measure in measures) {
    data_subset <- AD[, c(variable, measure)]
    formula <- as.formula(paste(measure, "~ ."))
    anova_res <- aov(formula, data = data_subset)
    tukey_result <- HSD.test(anova_res, variable)
    print(tukey_result)
  }
}
## $statistics

Let’s visualize alpha diversity results with rarefaction curve

sample_data <- data.frame(sample_data(sod_prunereads.ps))
otu_data <- as(otu_table(sod_prunereads.ps), "matrix")
sample_data$Fire_Mod <- factor(sample_data$Fire_Mod, levels = c("Untreated", "Treated", "Wildfire"))

colors <- ifelse(sample_data$Fire_Mod == "Untreated", "blue",
                 ifelse(sample_data$Fire_Mod == "Treated", "red", "green"))

raremax <- min(rowSums(otu_data))

pdf("Figures/RPS10/rarefaction_curve_wildfire_samples.pdf", width = 10, height = 8)
rarecurve(otu_data, step = 10, sample = raremax, col = colors, cex = 0.6, label = FALSE, xlab = "Sequencing Depth", ylab = "Number of ASVs")
legend("topright", legend = levels(sample_data$Fire_Mod), col = c("blue", "red", "green"), lty = 1, title = "Treatuntreat_EP")
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2
sod_prunereads.ps@sam_data$Fire_Mod 
##  [1] Untreated Untreated Untreated Wildfire  Wildfire  Wildfire  Wildfire 
##  [8] Wildfire  Untreated Untreated Untreated Untreated Untreated Untreated
## [15] Untreated Untreated Untreated Untreated Untreated Untreated Untreated
## [22] Untreated Wildfire  Untreated Untreated Untreated Untreated Treated  
## [29] Wildfire  Wildfire  Wildfire  Untreated Untreated Treated   Untreated
## [36] Treated   Treated   Treated   Untreated Treated   Untreated Untreated
## [43] Untreated Untreated Treated   Untreated Untreated Untreated Treated  
## [50] Treated   Treated   Treated   Treated   Wildfire  Wildfire  Wildfire 
## [57] Wildfire  Treated   Treated   Treated   Treated   Treated   Untreated
## [64] Untreated Untreated Untreated Treated   Treated   Treated   Treated  
## [71] Treated   Treated   Treated   Treated   Treated   Treated   Treated  
## [78] Treated   Treated   Treated   Treated   Untreated Untreated Treated  
## [85] Treated   Treated   Treated  
## Levels: Untreated Treated Wildfire

Let’s also perform Kruskal-Wallis and Dunn’s Post-hoc tests in case our data violates key ANOVA assumptions

set.seed(1)
variables <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")
measures <- c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon")

final_kw_table <- data.table(factor_name = character(), alpha_index = character(),
                              chi_squared = numeric(), df = numeric(), pvalue = numeric())

final_dunn_table <- data.table(factor_name = character(), alpha_index = character(),
                                Comparison = character(), Z = numeric(),
                                P.unadj = numeric(), P.adj = numeric())

for (variable in variables) {
  for (measure in measures) {
    data_subset <- AD[, c(variable, measure)]
    formula <- as.formula(paste(measure, "~", variable))

    cat("\n=====", measure, "by", variable, "=====\n")
    kw_res <- kruskal.test(formula, data = data_subset)
    print(kw_res)

    final_kw_table <- rbind(final_kw_table, data.table(
      factor_name = variable, alpha_index = measure,
      chi_squared = unname(kw_res$statistic), df = unname(kw_res$parameter),
      pvalue = kw_res$p.value
    ))

    n_groups <- nlevels(droplevels(data_subset[[variable]]))
    if (n_groups == 2) {
  
      cat("(2 groups -- Dunn's post-hoc skipped as not applicable)\n")
    } else {
      dunn_res <- FSA::dunnTest(formula, data = data_subset, method = "bh")
      print(dunn_res)

      dunn_table_sub <- dunn_res$res
      dunn_table_sub$factor_name <- variable
      dunn_table_sub$alpha_index <- measure
      dunn_table_sub <- dunn_table_sub[, c("factor_name", "alpha_index", "Comparison", "Z", "P.unadj", "P.adj")]

      final_dunn_table <- rbind(final_dunn_table, dunn_table_sub)
    }
  }
}
## 
## ===== Observed by Treatuntreat_EP =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  Observed by Treatuntreat_EP
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 0.025249, df = 1, p-value = 0.8737
## 
## (2 groups -- Dunn's post-hoc skipped as not applicable)
## 
## ===== InvSimpson by Treatuntreat_EP =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  InvSimpson by Treatuntreat_EP
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 0.057416, df = 1, p-value = 0.8106
## 
## (2 groups -- Dunn's post-hoc skipped as not applicable)
## 
## ===== Shannon by Treatuntreat_EP =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  Shannon by Treatuntreat_EP
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 0.045772, df = 1, p-value = 0.8306
## 
## (2 groups -- Dunn's post-hoc skipped as not applicable)
## 
## ===== Observed by Fire_Mod =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  Observed by Fire_Mod
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 6.3699, df = 2, p-value = 0.04138
## Registered S3 methods overwritten by 'FSA':
##   method       from
##   confint.boot car 
##   hist.boot    car
##   Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data: x and g
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 6.3699, df = 2, p-value = 0.04
## 
## 
##                      Dunn's Pairwise Comparison of x by g                     
##                              (Benjamini-Hochberg)                             
## Col Mean-│
## Row Mean │    Treated   Untreate
## ─────────┼──────────────────────
## Untreate │   1.077401
##          │     0.2813 
##          │
## Wildfire │   2.518860   1.756751
##          │     0.0353*    0.1184 
## 
## FDR = 0.05
## Reject Ho if p ≤ FDR with stopping rule, where p = Pr(|Z| ≥ |z|)
## Dunn (1964) Kruskal-Wallis multiple comparison
## 
##   p-values adjusted with the Benjamini-Hochberg method.
##             Comparison        Z    P.unadj      P.adj
## 1  Treated - Untreated 1.077401 0.28130112 0.28130112
## 2   Treated - Wildfire 2.518861 0.01177352 0.03532056
## 3 Untreated - Wildfire 1.756752 0.07896015 0.11844022
## 
## ===== InvSimpson by Fire_Mod =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  InvSimpson by Fire_Mod
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 7.1867, df = 2, p-value = 0.02751
##   Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data: x and g
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 7.1867, df = 2, p-value = 0.03
## 
## 
##                      Dunn's Pairwise Comparison of x by g                     
##                              (Benjamini-Hochberg)                             
## Col Mean-│
## Row Mean │    Treated   Untreate
## ─────────┼──────────────────────
## Untreate │   1.208231
##          │     0.2270 
##          │
## Wildfire │   2.670066   1.814322
##          │     0.0228*    0.1044 
## 
## FDR = 0.05
## Reject Ho if p ≤ FDR with stopping rule, where p = Pr(|Z| ≥ |z|)
## Dunn (1964) Kruskal-Wallis multiple comparison
## 
##   p-values adjusted with the Benjamini-Hochberg method.
##             Comparison        Z    P.unadj      P.adj
## 1  Treated - Untreated 1.208231 0.22695841 0.22695841
## 2   Treated - Wildfire 2.670066 0.00758363 0.02275089
## 3 Untreated - Wildfire 1.814322 0.06962815 0.10444222
## 
## ===== Shannon by Fire_Mod =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  Shannon by Fire_Mod
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 7.8186, df = 2, p-value = 0.02005
##   Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data: x and g
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 7.8186, df = 2, p-value = 0.02
## 
## 
##                      Dunn's Pairwise Comparison of x by g                     
##                              (Benjamini-Hochberg)                             
## Col Mean-│
## Row Mean │    Treated   Untreate
## ─────────┼──────────────────────
## Untreate │   1.227891
##          │     0.2195 
##          │
## Wildfire │   2.787974   1.918831
##          │     0.0159*    0.0825 
## 
## FDR = 0.05
## Reject Ho if p ≤ FDR with stopping rule, where p = Pr(|Z| ≥ |z|)
## Dunn (1964) Kruskal-Wallis multiple comparison
## 
##   p-values adjusted with the Benjamini-Hochberg method.
##             Comparison        Z     P.unadj      P.adj
## 1  Treated - Untreated 1.227891 0.219487807 0.21948781
## 2   Treated - Wildfire 2.787975 0.005303868 0.01591161
## 3 Untreated - Wildfire 1.918831 0.055005667 0.08250850
## 
## ===== Observed by YrSinceTrt_BR =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  Observed by YrSinceTrt_BR
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 1.7618, df = 4, p-value = 0.7795
##   Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data: x and g
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 1.7618, df = 4, p-value = 0.78
## 
## 
##                      Dunn's Pairwise Comparison of x by g                     
##                              (Benjamini-Hochberg)                             
## Col Mean-│
## Row Mean │     10to12       1to2       3to4       5to8
## ─────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────
##     1to2 │   1.279158
##          │     1.0000 
##          │
##     3to4 │   0.829000  -0.270338
##          │     1.0000     1.0000 
##          │
##     5to8 │   1.099033   0.045133   0.255691
##          │     0.9058     0.9640     1.0000 
##          │
## untreate │   1.197297  -0.198677   0.157441  -0.173720
##          │     1.0000     1.0000     0.9721     1.0000 
## 
## FDR = 0.05
## Reject Ho if p ≤ FDR with stopping rule, where p = Pr(|Z| ≥ |z|)
## Dunn (1964) Kruskal-Wallis multiple comparison
## 
##   p-values adjusted with the Benjamini-Hochberg method.
##            Comparison           Z   P.unadj     P.adj
## 1       10to12 - 1to2  1.27915899 0.2008411 1.0000000
## 2       10to12 - 3to4  0.82900055 0.4071041 1.0000000
## 3         1to2 - 3to4 -0.27033880 0.7868996 1.0000000
## 4       10to12 - 5to8  1.09903377 0.2717533 0.9058445
## 5         1to2 - 5to8  0.04513375 0.9640007 0.9640007
## 6         3to4 - 5to8  0.25569116 0.7981893 1.0000000
## 7  10to12 - untreated  1.19729727 0.2311907 1.0000000
## 8    1to2 - untreated -0.19867792 0.8425147 1.0000000
## 9    3to4 - untreated  0.15744126 0.8748971 0.9721079
## 10   5to8 - untreated -0.17372061 0.8620850 1.0000000
## 
## ===== InvSimpson by YrSinceTrt_BR =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  InvSimpson by YrSinceTrt_BR
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 1.7577, df = 4, p-value = 0.7802
##   Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data: x and g
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 1.7577, df = 4, p-value = 0.78
## 
## 
##                      Dunn's Pairwise Comparison of x by g                     
##                              (Benjamini-Hochberg)                             
## Col Mean-│
## Row Mean │     10to12       1to2       3to4       5to8
## ─────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────
##     1to2 │   0.031430
##          │     0.9749 
##          │
##     3to4 │   0.981006   1.258095
##          │     1.0000     1.0000 
##          │
##     5to8 │   0.339045   0.421475  -0.700756
##          │     0.9182     1.0000     1.0000 
##          │
## untreate │   0.381832   0.616717  -0.919128  -0.039505
##          │     1.0000     1.0000     1.0000     1.0000 
## 
## FDR = 0.05
## Reject Ho if p ≤ FDR with stopping rule, where p = Pr(|Z| ≥ |z|)
## Dunn (1964) Kruskal-Wallis multiple comparison
## 
##   p-values adjusted with the Benjamini-Hochberg method.
##            Comparison           Z   P.unadj     P.adj
## 1       10to12 - 1to2  0.03143018 0.9749265 0.9749265
## 2       10to12 - 3to4  0.98100654 0.3265895 1.0000000
## 3         1to2 - 3to4  1.25809515 0.2083573 1.0000000
## 4       10to12 - 5to8  0.33904551 0.7345754 0.9182193
## 5         1to2 - 5to8  0.42147546 0.6734079 1.0000000
## 6         3to4 - 5to8 -0.70075690 0.4834547 1.0000000
## 7  10to12 - untreated  0.38183262 0.7025855 1.0000000
## 8    1to2 - untreated  0.61671792 0.5374208 1.0000000
## 9    3to4 - untreated -0.91912865 0.3580283 1.0000000
## 10   5to8 - untreated -0.03950535 0.9684875 1.0000000
## 
## ===== Shannon by YrSinceTrt_BR =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  Shannon by YrSinceTrt_BR
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 1.4147, df = 4, p-value = 0.8416
##   Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data: x and g
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 1.4147, df = 4, p-value = 0.84
## 
## 
##                      Dunn's Pairwise Comparison of x by g                     
##                              (Benjamini-Hochberg)                             
## Col Mean-│
## Row Mean │     10to12       1to2       3to4       5to8
## ─────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────
##     1to2 │   0.365637
##          │     0.7940 
##          │
##     3to4 │   1.040307   0.980377
##          │     1.0000     1.0000 
##          │
##     5to8 │   0.610892   0.412657  -0.480831
##          │     1.0000     0.8498     1.0000 
##          │
## untreate │   0.630063   0.450885  -0.734217  -0.136594
##          │     1.0000     0.9315     1.0000     0.8914 
## 
## FDR = 0.05
## Reject Ho if p ≤ FDR with stopping rule, where p = Pr(|Z| ≥ |z|)
## Dunn (1964) Kruskal-Wallis multiple comparison
## 
##   p-values adjusted with the Benjamini-Hochberg method.
##            Comparison          Z   P.unadj     P.adj
## 1       10to12 - 1to2  0.3656377 0.7146354 0.7940394
## 2       10to12 - 3to4  1.0403075 0.2981971 1.0000000
## 3         1to2 - 3to4  0.9803773 0.3268999 1.0000000
## 4       10to12 - 5to8  0.6108928 0.5412705 1.0000000
## 5         1to2 - 5to8  0.4126580 0.6798572 0.8498215
## 6         3to4 - 5to8 -0.4808312 0.6306364 1.0000000
## 7  10to12 - untreated  0.6300633 0.5286531 1.0000000
## 8    1to2 - untreated  0.4508853 0.6520722 0.9315317
## 9    3to4 - untreated -0.7342173 0.4628163 1.0000000
## 10   5to8 - untreated -0.1365948 0.8913511 0.8913511
print(knitr::kable(final_kw_table, format = "markdown"))
## 
## 
## |factor_name     |alpha_index | chi_squared| df|    pvalue|
## |:---------------|:-----------|-----------:|--:|---------:|
## |Treatuntreat_EP |Observed    |   0.0252485|  1| 0.8737494|
## |Treatuntreat_EP |InvSimpson  |   0.0574163|  1| 0.8106272|
## |Treatuntreat_EP |Shannon     |   0.0457719|  1| 0.8305910|
## |Fire_Mod        |Observed    |   6.3699082|  2| 0.0413801|
## |Fire_Mod        |InvSimpson  |   7.1866697|  2| 0.0275064|
## |Fire_Mod        |Shannon     |   7.8185749|  2| 0.0200548|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR   |Observed    |   1.7617532|  4| 0.7794719|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR   |InvSimpson  |   1.7576794|  4| 0.7802154|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR   |Shannon     |   1.4146996|  4| 0.8416362|
write.csv(final_kw_table, "Results/RPS10/final_kruskalwallis_table_wildfire_samples.csv", row.names = FALSE)

print(knitr::kable(final_dunn_table, format = "markdown"))
## 
## 
## |factor_name   |alpha_index |Comparison           |          Z|   P.unadj|     P.adj|
## |:-------------|:-----------|:--------------------|----------:|---------:|---------:|
## |Fire_Mod      |Observed    |Treated - Untreated  |  1.0774011| 0.2813011| 0.2813011|
## |Fire_Mod      |Observed    |Treated - Wildfire   |  2.5188608| 0.0117735| 0.0353206|
## |Fire_Mod      |Observed    |Untreated - Wildfire |  1.7567517| 0.0789601| 0.1184402|
## |Fire_Mod      |InvSimpson  |Treated - Untreated  |  1.2082310| 0.2269584| 0.2269584|
## |Fire_Mod      |InvSimpson  |Treated - Wildfire   |  2.6700662| 0.0075836| 0.0227509|
## |Fire_Mod      |InvSimpson  |Untreated - Wildfire |  1.8143221| 0.0696281| 0.1044422|
## |Fire_Mod      |Shannon     |Treated - Untreated  |  1.2278912| 0.2194878| 0.2194878|
## |Fire_Mod      |Shannon     |Treated - Wildfire   |  2.7879747| 0.0053039| 0.0159116|
## |Fire_Mod      |Shannon     |Untreated - Wildfire |  1.9188315| 0.0550057| 0.0825085|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |10to12 - 1to2        |  1.2791590| 0.2008411| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |10to12 - 3to4        |  0.8290005| 0.4071041| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |1to2 - 3to4          | -0.2703388| 0.7868996| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |10to12 - 5to8        |  1.0990338| 0.2717533| 0.9058445|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |1to2 - 5to8          |  0.0451338| 0.9640007| 0.9640007|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |3to4 - 5to8          |  0.2556912| 0.7981893| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |10to12 - untreated   |  1.1972973| 0.2311907| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |1to2 - untreated     | -0.1986779| 0.8425147| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |3to4 - untreated     |  0.1574413| 0.8748971| 0.9721079|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |5to8 - untreated     | -0.1737206| 0.8620850| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |10to12 - 1to2        |  0.0314302| 0.9749265| 0.9749265|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |10to12 - 3to4        |  0.9810065| 0.3265895| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |1to2 - 3to4          |  1.2580951| 0.2083573| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |10to12 - 5to8        |  0.3390455| 0.7345754| 0.9182193|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |1to2 - 5to8          |  0.4214755| 0.6734079| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |3to4 - 5to8          | -0.7007569| 0.4834547| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |10to12 - untreated   |  0.3818326| 0.7025855| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |1to2 - untreated     |  0.6167179| 0.5374208| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |3to4 - untreated     | -0.9191286| 0.3580283| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |5to8 - untreated     | -0.0395054| 0.9684875| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |10to12 - 1to2        |  0.3656377| 0.7146354| 0.7940394|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |10to12 - 3to4        |  1.0403075| 0.2981971| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |1to2 - 3to4          |  0.9803773| 0.3268999| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |10to12 - 5to8        |  0.6108928| 0.5412705| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |1to2 - 5to8          |  0.4126580| 0.6798572| 0.8498215|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |3to4 - 5to8          | -0.4808312| 0.6306364| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |10to12 - untreated   |  0.6300633| 0.5286531| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |1to2 - untreated     |  0.4508853| 0.6520722| 0.9315317|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |3to4 - untreated     | -0.7342173| 0.4628163| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |5to8 - untreated     | -0.1365948| 0.8913511| 0.8913511|
write.csv(final_dunn_table, "Results/RPS10/final_dunns_posthoc_table_wildfire_samples.csv", row.names = FALSE)

Beta diversity-PERMANOVA analyses-ASV counts transformed (rel. abundance)

set.seed(1)
mapping <- as.data.frame(sample_data(strat_rel.ps))

mapping2=mapping[, c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")]

ASV_tables_bray <- phyloseq::distance(strat_rel.ps, method = "bray")

df_metadata <- data.frame(sample_data(strat_rel.ps))

df_metadata<-df_metadata[, c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")]

pvals <- sapply(colnames(mapping2), function(variable){
  form1<-as.formula(paste("ASV_tables_bray", variable, sep="~"))
  res <- adonis2(form1, data = df_metadata, permutations = 9999)
  pval <- res$`Pr(>F)`[[1]] 
  return(pval)
})

pval_adj <- p.adjust(pvals, method = "fdr")
save.pval.adonis0.05_braycurtis <- pval_adj[pval_adj < 0.05]
save.pval.adonis0.05_braycurtis
## Fire_Mod 
##   0.0015
print(pval_adj)
## Treatuntreat_EP        Fire_Mod   YrSinceTrt_BR 
##         0.07460         0.00150         0.05685

Compile PERMANOVA stats-main effects

set.seed(1)
res <- adonis2(ASV_tables_bray ~ Fire_Mod, data = df_metadata, permutations = 9999, by="terms")
res_data <- data.frame(res)
colnames(res_data)[colnames(res_data) == "Pr..F."] <- "pr_f"
res_data <- res_data[-c(nrow(res_data)-1, nrow(res_data)),]

res_data$FDR_adj_p <- p.adjust(res_data$pr_f, method = "fdr")
res_data$Bonferroni_adj_p <- p.adjust(res_data$pr_f, method = "bonferroni")
res_data$Factor <- rownames(res_data)

res_data <- res_data[, c("Factor", "Df", "SumOfSqs", "R2", "F", "pr_f", "FDR_adj_p", "Bonferroni_adj_p")]
rownames(res_data) <- NULL

beta_disp_results <- lapply(c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod"), function(variable) {
  res_betadisper <- betadisper(d = ASV_tables_bray, group = df_metadata[[variable]])
  perm_test_res <- permutest(res_betadisper)
  pval_betadisper <- perm_test_res$tab$`Pr(>F)`[[1]]
  
  data.frame(Factor = variable, P_Value_BetaDispersion = pval_betadisper)
})

beta_disp_results_df <- do.call(rbind, beta_disp_results)

final_results <- merge(res_data, beta_disp_results_df, by = "Factor", all.x = TRUE)

final_results <- final_results[, c("Factor", "Df", "SumOfSqs", "R2", "F", "pr_f", "FDR_adj_p", "Bonferroni_adj_p", "P_Value_BetaDispersion")]

print(final_results, row.names = FALSE)
##    Factor Df SumOfSqs         R2        F  pr_f FDR_adj_p Bonferroni_adj_p
##  Fire_Mod  2 1.611619 0.03979095 1.740475 3e-04     3e-04            3e-04
##  P_Value_BetaDispersion
##                   0.017
write.csv(final_results, file = "Results/RPS10/PERMANOVA_with_betadisper_maineffects_wildfire_samples.csv", row.names = FALSE)

Let’s now examine beta diversity through ordination plots

We’ll will examine outputs for rarefied data as well as data adjusted by relative abundance We’ll now make ordination plots on data for which we’ve calculated relative abundance

set.seed(1)
ps_NMDS = ordinate(strat_rel.ps, 
                   method="NMDS", 
                   distance="bray")
## Run 0 stress 0.109223 
## Run 1 stress 0.1131808 
## Run 2 stress 0.1123609 
## Run 3 stress 0.1123088 
## Run 4 stress 0.1086841 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.08982218  max resid 0.2672634 
## Run 5 stress 0.1089209 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.09246837  max resid 0.3137624 
## Run 6 stress 0.1097789 
## Run 7 stress 0.1077027 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.05501745  max resid 0.2685993 
## Run 8 stress 0.1108835 
## Run 9 stress 0.1123249 
## Run 10 stress 0.1086097 
## Run 11 stress 0.1091643 
## Run 12 stress 0.1095843 
## Run 13 stress 0.1109532 
## Run 14 stress 0.1135691 
## Run 15 stress 0.1105727 
## Run 16 stress 0.1087824 
## Run 17 stress 0.1090169 
## Run 18 stress 0.1151811 
## Run 19 stress 0.108699 
## Run 20 stress 0.1100657 
## *** Best solution was not repeated -- monoMDS stopping criteria:
##     20: no. of iterations >= maxit
plot<- plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, 
                         ps_NMDS,
                         color= "Treatuntreat_EP") +
  ggtitle("NMDS ordination plot - relative abundance") +
  labs(color = "Factor") + 
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14), axis.title=element_text(size=14), title =  element_text(size=13), legend.text = element_text(size = 18))

plot_ellipses<-plot +
   stat_ellipse(type = "norm", linetype = 2) +
  theme_bw()

plot_ellipses

write.csv(ps_NMDS[["points"]], file="Results/RPS10/RPS10_NMDS_coordinates_persample_wildfire_samples.csv",row.names = TRUE)

Now, we’ll look at ordination plot (rel abundance dataset), with Fire_Mod factor

set.seed(1)
ps_NMDS = ordinate(strat_rel.ps, 
                   method="NMDS",
                   distance="bray")
## Run 0 stress 0.109223 
## Run 1 stress 0.1131808 
## Run 2 stress 0.1123609 
## Run 3 stress 0.1123088 
## Run 4 stress 0.1086841 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.08982218  max resid 0.2672634 
## Run 5 stress 0.1089209 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.09246837  max resid 0.3137624 
## Run 6 stress 0.1097789 
## Run 7 stress 0.1077027 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.05501745  max resid 0.2685993 
## Run 8 stress 0.1108835 
## Run 9 stress 0.1123249 
## Run 10 stress 0.1086097 
## Run 11 stress 0.1091643 
## Run 12 stress 0.1095843 
## Run 13 stress 0.1109532 
## Run 14 stress 0.1135691 
## Run 15 stress 0.1105727 
## Run 16 stress 0.1087824 
## Run 17 stress 0.1090169 
## Run 18 stress 0.1151811 
## Run 19 stress 0.108699 
## Run 20 stress 0.1100657 
## *** Best solution was not repeated -- monoMDS stopping criteria:
##     20: no. of iterations >= maxit
plot<- plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, 
                         ps_NMDS,
                         labs(color = "Factor"),
                         color= "Fire_Mod") +
  ggtitle("NMDS ordination plot - relative abundance") +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14), axis.title=element_text(size=14), title =  element_text(size=13), legend.text = element_text(size = 18))
## Warning in plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, ps_NMDS, labs(color = "Factor"), : type argument not supported. `type` set to 'samples'.
## See `plot_ordination('list')`
plot_ellipses<-plot +
   stat_ellipse(type = "norm", linetype = 2) +
  theme_bw()

plot_ellipses

Now, we’ll look at ordination plot rel abundance-with YrSinceTrt_BR factor

set.seed(1)
ps_NMDS = ordinate(strat_rel.ps, 
                   method="NMDS",
                   distance="bray")
## Run 0 stress 0.109223 
## Run 1 stress 0.1131808 
## Run 2 stress 0.1123609 
## Run 3 stress 0.1123088 
## Run 4 stress 0.1086841 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.08982218  max resid 0.2672634 
## Run 5 stress 0.1089209 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.09246837  max resid 0.3137624 
## Run 6 stress 0.1097789 
## Run 7 stress 0.1077027 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.05501745  max resid 0.2685993 
## Run 8 stress 0.1108835 
## Run 9 stress 0.1123249 
## Run 10 stress 0.1086097 
## Run 11 stress 0.1091643 
## Run 12 stress 0.1095843 
## Run 13 stress 0.1109532 
## Run 14 stress 0.1135691 
## Run 15 stress 0.1105727 
## Run 16 stress 0.1087824 
## Run 17 stress 0.1090169 
## Run 18 stress 0.1151811 
## Run 19 stress 0.108699 
## Run 20 stress 0.1100657 
## *** Best solution was not repeated -- monoMDS stopping criteria:
##     20: no. of iterations >= maxit
plot<- plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, 
                         ps_NMDS,
                         labs(color = "Factor"),
                         color= "YrSinceTrt_BR") +
  ggtitle("NMDS ordination plot - relative abundance") +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14), axis.title=element_text(size=14), title =  element_text(size=13), legend.text = element_text(size = 18))
## Warning in plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, ps_NMDS, labs(color = "Factor"), : type argument not supported. `type` set to 'samples'.
## See `plot_ordination('list')`
plot_ellipses<-plot +
   stat_ellipse(type = "norm", linetype = 2) +
  theme_bw()

plot_ellipses

### Software used

sessioninfo::session_info()
## ─ Session info ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
##  setting  value
##  version  R version 4.4.2 (2024-10-31)
##  os       macOS Sequoia 15.1
##  system   aarch64, darwin20
##  ui       X11
##  language (EN)
##  collate  en_US.UTF-8
##  ctype    en_US.UTF-8
##  tz       America/Los_Angeles
##  date     2026-07-13
##  pandoc   3.2 @ /Applications/RStudio.app/Contents/Resources/app/quarto/bin/tools/aarch64/ (via rmarkdown)
##  quarto   1.3.450 @ /usr/local/bin/quarto
## 
## ─ Packages ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
##  package              * version  date (UTC) lib source
##  abind                  1.4-8    2024-09-12 [2] CRAN (R 4.4.1)
##  ade4                   1.7-23   2025-02-14 [2] CRAN (R 4.4.1)
##  agricolae            * 1.3-7    2023-10-22 [2] CRAN (R 4.4.0)
